raw_httpx.title: Holgersen Bilsyn | Nem og hurtig online booking

gdpr_reasons: Visitor tracking: Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager

findings.description: Missing HSTS header (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
findings.risk: Browsers are not instructed to always use HTTPS. On unsecured networks (public WiFi), a visitor's first connection could be intercepted before the redirect to HTTPS occurs.

findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser has no restrictions on which scripts can run on the page. If the site is compromised, injected scripts can operate without constraint.

findings.description: Missing X-Frame-Options header
findings.risk: The website can be embedded in frames on other sites. This enables clickjacking attacks where users interact with hidden elements overlaid on the legitimate page.

findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header
findings.risk: Browsers may misinterpret uploaded files as executable content. This is primarily relevant if the site accepts file uploads.

findings.description: WordPress version 6.9.4 publicly disclosed
findings.risk: The exact WordPress version is visible to anyone viewing the page source. This allows attackers to look up known vulnerabilities specific to this version and target them directly.

findings.description: Data-handling plugins detected: Woocommerce, Contact Form 7
findings.risk: These plugins collect or process user data (form submissions, bookings, payments). If the site or plugin has a vulnerability, this data could be exposed. Keeping these plugins updated is critical for GDPR compliance.

findings.description: 6 WordPress plugins detected
findings.risk: Each plugin is additional code from a third-party developer. Outdated or abandoned plugins are a common entry point for attackers. Plugins should be reviewed and kept updated.

findings.description: Backend technology exposed: MySQL, PHP:8.4.19
findings.risk: The server advertises which backend technologies it runs. This gives attackers information about which exploits may be applicable. It does not mean the site is vulnerable, but it reduces the effort needed to find an attack vector.

findings.description: WordPress themes detected: Flatsome, Pavabilsyn Vejle
findings.risk: The active theme is visible in the HTML source. Outdated themes are a common source of vulnerabilities, similar to plugins.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Pixelyoursite (installed 0.2.3, latest 11.2.0.4)
findings.risk: The installed version of Pixelyoursite is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.

findings.description: PixelYourSite – Your smart PIXEL (TAG) & API Manager [pixelyoursite] < 5.3.0 (CVE-2018-0578)
findings.risk: CVE-2018-0578: WordPress plugin "PixelYourSite" vulnerable to cross-site scripting
